title: myrrhidium senecioides
intro: Myrrhidium senecioides, also known as prickly poppy or prickly cabbage, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It is a member of the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves, and small white or pink flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Myrrhidium senecioides include relief from coughs and colds, and its herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
